As I mentioned above work security is a major pros of this company, but it is somehow impacting various things at here. People are at their comfort zone, they have developed the status quo and are hardly open to any change. Young employees are struggling to find a meaningful role in the organization. Employees are spending 15-20 years in one role, then how will the doors open for young employees? They just have to do back-office work and MIS work, that's how things are. 
Also, technology adoption is very minimal, there is hardly any opportunity to learn new software and tools, as we are supposed to do everything manually, and because of that every day everyone is doing repetitive tasks day in and day out.
